name: event-driven
description: Event-Driven Architecture: event versioning, idempotency, dead-letter handling.

rules.guidance: Events include a version field. Schema changes are additive/backward-compatible. Breaking changes require a new event type or version, not modification of existing consumers.
All event consumers must be idempotent. Use event IDs + deduplication store or natural idempotency keys. Processing the same event twice must produce the same end state.
Unprocessable events are routed to a dead-letter queue/topic with the original payload, error reason, and retry count. Silently discarding events is forbidden.
Do not assume global event ordering unless your broker guarantees it per partition/key. Design consumers to tolerate out-of-order delivery or use ordering keys explicitly.
